topic: HOL-STRESSTEST c2 falsifiability-hardening — 5-patch expansion proposal targeting docs/index.html (OPERATOR-APPLY)
our_argument_summary: EXP-451 proposes 5 prose-layer patches (P1–P5) plus 3 deferred items, mapping onto the 5 holes from HOLISTIC-HOL-STRESSTEST.c2 (2026-05-30). P1 reword Overview hero thesis to match verdict architecture (ISS-2285). P2 add Schumann 22.6 Hz worked example after eg-falsifiability Condition 1 (ISS-2284). P3a rewrite ds-fm-caption to pre-commitment framing + P3b add ~1 nT discriminating-window detail to C2 status sublabel (ISS-2284). P4 inline operational definition for 4 silent failures in accuracy-math-section (ISS-2286). P5 reframe Overview stance statement as conclusion-from-evidence (ISS-2286 + overlap with HOL-NARRATIVE c2). Three deferred items: verdict-category placeholder (DEFER-01), module-heading rename (DEFER-02), living-document acknowledgment (DEFER-03) — all operator-decision requiring. Verdict architecture unchanged.

proposal_review.verdict_flip_plausibility: None. EXP-451 is presentation-layer hardening only. No WIN verdict changes; all 70 verdicts preserved. The hero rewording (P1) softens a sweeping claim but does not move the Survive-Scrutiny count (still 0/70). Closing the falsifiability optic without conceding any verdict is the explicit design goal. ✓
proposal_review.new_tag_naming: N/A (no code_analysis tags affected). The patches touch prose anchors only — ds-thesis-statement, ds-sc-sublabel, ds-fm-caption, ds-fm-status row, accuracy-math-section.ams-p, ds-stance-statement, eg-falsifiability ps-detail.

alternatives_considered.P1: Analyst considered the longer 18-non-discriminating + 52-dome-specific split and rejected for less punchy. Reasonable tradeoff for an Overview hero.
alternatives_considered.P3: Analyst chose caption rewrite + sublabel detail-surfacing INSTEAD OF the c2 architectural alternatives (add 5th open-ended condition; rename module heading). Defensible — DEFER-02 names the heading-rename for separate operator decision.
alternatives_considered.P5: Analyst chose option (a) — rewrite stance to conclusion-from-evidence — over option (b) — move stance below the verdict bars. Preserves rhetorical force; partially addresses the procedural critique.

proposal_review.unknowns_acknowledged: Three deferred items explicitly flagged: DEFER-01 verdict-category placeholder (defer until eclipse resolves Aug 2026), DEFER-02 module-heading rename (rhetorical tradeoff), DEFER-03 living-document acknowledgment (attack-surface tradeoff). Cross-reference overlap with HOL-NARRATIVE c2 (P5 closes both) and HOL-TAXONOMY c2 (DEFER-01) called out. ✓

patch_find_text_audit.method: grep against docs/index.html in fresh clone after git pull --rebase; substring match required for each patch find_text

results.find_text_sublabel: NOT FOUND — actual sublabel wraps 'What would change this?' in …; patch find_text omits the anchor tag. Patch either fails to apply or strips the cross-tab link.

results.line_drift: -9 (actual 669, claim 678) — large drift, suggests stale source build

results.find_text: FOUND (substring on line 436, ds-fm-caption). The replace_text omits the trailing Full criteria → jump link, but because the find_text is a strict substring of the line, the trailing anchor will be preserved under literal substring substitution. ✓

results.aria_label_not_updated: The same row carries aria-label="Status: 1 partial; eclipse August 12, 2026 approaches the standard with methodological issues" — patch updates the visible sub-span but not the aria-label, so screen-reader users miss the ~1 nT detail.

results.find_text: FOUND (exact substring on line 497, accuracy-math-section ams-p). However replace_text invents a [REMOVED] / [SUSPENDED] bracket-marker convention NOT USED on the dome's actual site — see hole #2 below.

c2_hole_to_patch_coverage.c2_hole_0_falsifiability_module_tautolog: Addressed by P2 (worked example demonstrates operationalizability) + P3a (caption pre-commitment reframe) + P3b (C2 sublabel discriminating-window detail surfaced). Architecture-level alternatives (5th open-ended condition; module rename) deferred to DEFER-02. Prose-layer mitigation is partial — the four-badge zero stack remains visible, so the visual optic the c2 review flagged is not closed by prose alone. Patch set is necessary-but-not-sufficient unless DEFER-02 also lands eventually.
c2_hole_to_patch_coverage.c2_hole_1_hero_thesis_conflation: Addressed by P1 (rewording). Direct fix to the c2 hole. Sublabel-change subpatch has a structural defect (see hole #1 below).
c2_hole_to_patch_coverage.c2_hole_2_stance_vs_principle3: Addressed by P5 (conclusion-from-evidence reframing). Partial — chooses prose mitigation (option a) over re-ordering (option b). Acceptable design choice given the operator preference for stance-up-front.
c2_hole_to_patch_coverage.c2_hole_3_silent_failures_denominator: Addressed by P4 (inline operational definition). BUT the replace_text manufactures a bracket-marker convention the dome does not use — see hole #2 below.
c2_hole_to_patch_coverage.c2_hole_4_living_document_optic: Deferred to DEFER-03. Analyst recommends keep deferred. Reasonable.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — proposal review of prose patches, no monitor.py or dome-code calculation involved.
data_sources_used: none for patches; existing review prose cross-checked
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: P1 sublabel anchor mismatch and P4 bracket-marker fabrication are the two escape clauses a dome defender can exploit.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: P4 replace_text claims dome 'visibly marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]' — grep of raw-text confirms: zero occurrences of '[REMOVED]'; 'SUSPENDED' appears once as a section header '=== SUSPENDED — Under Revision ===' in 03-predictions.txt, not as a bracket-marker convention. The dome's actual surface signals are 'FALSIFIED', 'refined', 'PENDING-PROSPECTIVE', and prose-level removal actions, all of which the existing review already documents in p3-ack-failures and p3-silent-failures.

holes_found.description: P1.sublabel_change find_text does NOT MATCH the actual content of the ds-sc-sublabel for 'Survive Scrutiny' (line 704). The actual text wraps 'What would change this?' in an … anchor tag. The patch find_text omits the anchor entirely. If the operator applies the patch verbatim with strict substring matching, no replacement happens. If applied with a fuzzy match that ignores the anchor, the cross-tab navigation link to the falsifiability section is STRIPPED — degrading reader navigation and breaking the very link the c2 review wants more prominent.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite P1.sublabel_change.find_text and replace_text to either (a) include the full anchor in both, e.g. find_text ends with '…confirmed by measurement. What would change this?' and replace_text correspondingly preserves the anchor with the new lead sentence; OR (b) narrow find_text to the substring that ends BEFORE the anchor (e.g., 'None produces a dome-derived prediction that both differs from standard physics and is confirmed by measurement.') and replace it with the new sentence, leaving the anchor untouched.

holes_found.description: P4 replace_text manufactures a bracket-marker convention ('[REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]') that the dome does NOT use on its public surface. Raw-text grep: zero occurrences of '[REMOVED]'; 'SUSPENDED' appears only once as section header '=== SUSPENDED — Under Revision ===' in 03-predictions.txt — NOT as a bracket-marker. The dome's actual surface signals are 'FALSIFIED', 'refined to damping', 'refined', 'PENDING-PROSPECTIVE', and prose-level removal actions. The patch's stated criterion — 'visibly marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]' — is falsifiable against the dome's own page. A dome defender screenshotting his front page can demonstrate the bracket-tag convention does not exist, FLIPPING the symmetric-methodology argument the patch was designed to neutralize: instead of 'your denominator criterion is auditable on my surface,' the patch hands the defender 'your patch invents a marker convention I do not use.'
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite P4.replace_text to use criteria observable on the dome's actual surface. Two options: (1) the c2 reviewer's own suggestion — 'Predictions whose stated window-close date has passed by 14+ days with no result posted on the dome\'s page' — which is testable against the dome's prediction registry. (2) Name the four silent-failure entries by their dome-side history: e.g., 'The four silent failures are the four predictions the dome has visibly withdrawn or marked PENDING-PROSPECTIVE past its window-close date — each is named individually in the failure list below with the relevant dome-side action timestamp.' Either option keeps the operational definition INLINE without inventing surface signals the dome does not produce.

holes_found.description: P2 worked-example mischaracterizes the technical path to 22.6 Hz. The patch text says 'the dome cavity, treated as a flat-plate resonator with the published exponentially-decaying firmament profile, predicts a Schumann fundamental of 22.6 Hz from disc radius and firmament height alone.' But the existing review at line 1063 attributes 22.6 Hz to the LINEAR RADIAL AVERAGE H_eff = H₀·(L/R)·(1−exp(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km, then f = c/(4 × 3,316) — an averaging convention, not the natural cavity prediction. The dome's H(0) = 8,537 km in the same f = c/(4H) gives 8.77 Hz (the pole-floor frequency already named at lines 1059 and 1063). A dome defender can argue the worked example demonstrates an averaging scheme rather than 'the dome's own geometry' — exactly the kind of formula-switching the review elsewhere criticizes the dome for.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) replace the 22.6 Hz value with the unambiguous pole-floor 8.77 Hz, and the fictional observed value with 8.77 Hz, so the worked example uses only H(0) in f = c/(4H) — no averaging convention required; OR (b) keep 22.6 Hz but explicitly name the integration scheme: '…the linear-radial-averaged effective height H_eff ≈ 3,316 km in the quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H_eff) predicts 22.6 Hz' — making the averaging convention explicit and matching the review's existing technical framing. Option (a) is cleaner because it removes a degree of freedom from the worked example.

holes_found.description: P3b updates the visible <span class="ds-fm-status-sub"> to add '~1 nT discriminating window' but does NOT update the surrounding role="status" aria-label on the same <div>, which still reads 'Status: 1 partial; eclipse August 12, 2026 approaches the standard with methodological issues'. Screen-reader users will hear the old wording without the ~1 nT detail; sighted users will see the new wording. Inconsistent accessibility.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sub-patch to P3b updating the aria-label on the same div (line 446) to include the discriminating-window detail. Suggested: aria-label='Status: 1 partial; eclipse August 12, 2026 with ~1 nT discriminating window — approaches the standard with methodological issues'.

holes_found.description: Anchor line-number drift for P2: claim says 'around line 678', actual end-of-Condition-1 paragraph is at line 669 — a 9-line upward drift suggests the analyst worked from a stale build of docs/index.html. Other patches drift by 0–2 lines (within normal build-time tolerance), but P2's 9-line drift is large enough to be worth flagging — the operator may want to pull fresh before applying, and the analyst's local build was likely not current.
holes_found.recommendation: Operator: pull origin/main fresh before applying P2 (which is a pure-insertion patch; insert_after_text still matches at line 669, so the patch will apply correctly, but the line-number anchor in the patch metadata should be updated to 669 for posterity).

holes_found.description: P3a moderate-style issue: the new caption 'Four pre-committed conditions — set before the data, satisfiable by any future dome derivation. We will upgrade the relevant verdict if any one is met.' is correct in framing but slightly weaker than the existing 'any one is sufficient to upgrade a verdict' (which states sufficiency as fact); the new 'We will upgrade … if any one is met' is conditional commitment language. A dome defender can read 'if any one is met' as opt-out room ('what if you DON\'T upgrade?'). Net: the framing benefit outweighs the marginal sufficiency-language loss, but worth flagging.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish — consider 'Any one is sufficient to upgrade the relevant verdict' to retain the sufficiency assertion while keeping the pre-commitment framing.

stronger_arguments.description: EXP-451 closes the c2 falsifiability-module critique with prose-layer patches, but the c2 reviewer also flagged the visual four-badge zero stack as 'visually doubling the c1 rigged-framework optics.' P3a + P3b refresh the framing but do not address the visual itself. If DEFER-02 (heading rename) is also rejected, the next holistic review will re-flag this — the module's optical force comes from the four-zero stack independent of framing. A small additional patch — e.g., reorder the four conditions so C2 (eclipse, 1 partial) comes first, presenting a non-zero badge before the three zeros — would address the visual critique without architectural change. Operator decision.

P5 chose to reframe rather than re-order the stance statement. A complementary belt-and-braces move would be to add the audit-criteria link inline: '…applied through the audit criteria in the Evaluation Guide [link]; we do not assume it as a starting premise.' The current replace_text mentions 'audit criteria' but does not link to the Evaluation Guide tab — making it hard for the casual reader to verify the conclusion-from-evidence claim. Adding the link is a small edit that materially closes the procedural objection.

For P4, the cleanest dome-defender-resistant operational definition is the COMBINATION of (a) named entries (the four entries already listed in p3-silent-failures) AND (b) the dome-side action that constitutes 'removal' for each — visible-edit-removed-from-active-WIN-list (FAIL-007, FAIL-008) and PENDING-PROSPECTIVE-past-window-close (FAIL-009, FAIL-010, FAIL-011). The patch should reference both surface signals explicitly rather than invent a bracket-tag convention. The existing prose at line 2668 already gives the three-layer 'refined/suspended/removed' framing — P4 could simply DEEPEN that framing inline rather than introduce a new marker scheme.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-451 correctly identifies the c2 critique structure (presentation-layer optics rather than verdict-architecture defect) and the 5 patches map cleanly onto the 5 c2 holes (EXP-451 patches 1:1 with HOLISTIC-HOL-STRESSTEST.c2.json holes_found indices). The kernel-of-truth in the EXP itself (EXP-451:kernel_of_truth) is sharp: it concedes the dome defender's PROSE-VS-ARCHITECTURE distinction is valid, distinguishes 'Std Model Explains' from 'Refuted by Data' (the same distinction the Verdict Assignment Criteria section already makes), and acknowledges the conflation in the hero thesis. P1 directly fixes that conflation; P5 directly addresses the Principle-3 procedural objection raised in the c2 hole #2. The decision to DEFER the verdict-category placeholder (DEFER-01) until eclipse resolves (~Aug 2026, per env date 2026-05-30) is sound risk management — the placeholder would currently hold one entry that will either flip or empty in ~10 weeks, so shipping it now risks two-architecture-rewrites-in-two-months.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel does not save the patches from their two factual defects. P1.sublabel_change.find_text does not match line 704 of docs/index.html; P4.replace_text manufactures a [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] bracket convention not present on the dome's actual surface (verified by grep of raw-text/03-predictions.txt and the docs/index.html prose at line 2668 which uses the dome's actual three-layer refined/suspended/removed framing). Both are blocking for operator-apply: P1's sublabel either fails or strips the cross-tab anchor; P4 hands the dome defender a falsifiable claim that strengthens the very symmetric-methodology critique it was meant to neutralize. Neither defect is irreparable — both have straightforward fixes outlined in holes_found.recommendation. The substantive design of the proposal is sound; the execution has two land-mines.

recommendation_detail: Conditional approval: P3a, P3b (with aria-label fix), P5 can proceed as drafted. P1 needs find_text/replace_text revision for the sublabel anchor. P4 needs replace_text rewritten to remove the invented bracket convention. P2 needs the worked-example value either changed to 8.77 Hz pole-floor or annotated to name the linear-radial-average integration scheme. Three deferred items (DEFER-01/02/03) should be presented to the operator with the analyst's recommendations standing (defer/keep/defer respectively). No verdict change recommended.
summary_for_decider: EXP-451 is a sound presentation-layer hardening proposal mapped 1:1 onto the 5 holes from HOLISTIC-HOL-STRESSTEST.c2. Two patches need revision before operator-apply: (i) P1 sublabel_change.find_text does not include the <a href="#eg-falsifiability"…> anchor wrapping 'What would change this?' on line 704, so the patch will either fail to apply or strip the cross-tab link; (ii) P4 replace_text invents a [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] bracket-marker convention that does not exist on the dome's surface (grep-verified against raw-text/ and existing docs/index.html prose) and would hand the dome defender a falsifiable claim. Both have concrete fix recommendations in holes_found. P2 has a moderate technical sloppiness (22.6 Hz value attributed to 'flat-plate resonator' when it actually comes from the linear radial averaging of H(r); the dome's own H(0)=8537 km cavity gives 8.77 Hz). P3a (caption), P3b (sublabel visible-text only — aria-label needs minor fix), and P5 (stance reframe) can proceed as drafted. Three deferred items appropriately flagged for operator decision. No WIN verdict changes. Recommended action: minor_edit (decider self-applies the find_text/replace_text fixes to P1+P4, optionally P2 and P3b aria-label, then re-pushes to operator-apply queue). Cross-reference: P5 closes HOL-NARRATIVE c2 stance-statement hole too — coordinate with that EXP if separate.
deeper_analysis: The proposal demonstrates good adversarial discipline in the EXP authoring itself — the kernel_of_truth and anticipated_dome_objection fields show the analyst gamed out the dome-defender attack surface before writing the patches. The two factual defects (P1 sublabel anchor, P4 bracket convention) are not 'analyst was wrong about the argument' defects; they are 'analyst was working from a paraphrase rather than the live file' defects. The line-drift signature on P2 (-9 lines vs claim) supports the hypothesis that the analyst's local docs/index.html may have been slightly stale, so paraphrased substrings approximated the live content. For a deep-attack-class proposal at the cost of an Opus run, this is a recoverable error — decider self-apply can patch the patches. The bigger composite risk: if the operator applies P4 verbatim without seeing this review, the next dome-defender holistic critique (or even a casual reader auditing the dome's surface) finds the manufactured bracket convention and the c2 critique resurges with strengthened force. The blocking severity assignment is therefore correct. Verdict architecture stability is preserved throughout — no patch flips a verdict. The deferred items are well-reasoned, and the cross-reference overlap with HOL-NARRATIVE c2 (P5) and HOL-TAXONOMY c2 (DEFER-01) is correctly flagged for decider coordination. PROP-009 queue_id=421 set; queue_pushed_at copied verbatim.
